raw_consumer_statement: Back in XXXX, I booked a hotel room with XXXX for {$340.00} and I also paid an additional {$15.00} for XXXX XXXX insurance that covered a stay protection plan ( Policy effective date of XX/XX/year> - XXXX -- policy number XXXX ) that covered my stay 100 % for unforeseen issues ( ie inclement weather ). That weekend the governor declared the state of Virginia under a state of emergency due to the horrendous weather. Therefore, my work event was cancelled. I then reached out to XXXX XXXX and they told me what to submit for reimbursement -- however, over the following weeks no matter what I supplied to XXXX XXXX insurance plan -- they did not compensate me for my loss. So I went to my Citi Bank Credit card -- -where I have been a loyal customer for many years. I put in a dispute on XX/XX/XXXX and explained my situation. On XX/XX/XXXX Citibank contacted the Merchant. The merchant responded on XX/XX/XXXX and mainly sent them the XXXX booking that stated it was not refundable. CitiBank then stated I ( the customer ) was responsible. So I reached out to Citibank again and supplied them the XXXX XXXX insurance plan and various other documents. Then the dispute was reopened. And things went around and around again. I recently spoke to a customer rep of CitiBank and he mainly stated that XXXX XXXX might not even be a legit company. Bottom line -- CitiBank should remove this charge of {$340.00} whether XXXX XXXX is or is not a legitimate company.
